Engineers

BUSINESS
Efficient distributed team management at iDrift AS
Easy communication for 1000 agents at GUT contact
Managing hundreds of projects at End Point Dev
Worldwide operations and AI at WindBorne
More efficient communication than Slack at Semsee
Open distributed communication at Atolio
EDUCATION and RESEARCH
Organized chat for 1000s of students at TUM
Communication hub across 6 continents at UCSD
Connecting across generations at the National University of Córdoba
Research collaboration at scale in the Lean mathematical community
OPEN SOURCE and COMMUNITIES
Inclusive discussion in the open-source Asciidoctor community
Faster decision-making in the Rust language community
Platform for a worldwide community since 2013 at Recurse Center
Professional community support at Rush Stack
Collaboration drives innovation at Mixxx

GitHub
Pricing
Download
New organization
Log in
Organized chat for distributed teams
See how we use Zulip
No account needed
Create an organization
Anonymously if you like
Contact sales
Talk to a helpful human
Designed for async conversations.
Here's what it looks like in action.
Get an overview of conversations with unread messages in your inbox.
Focus on one conversation at a time, no matter how many others are happening.
Reply to each conversation in context.
Conversations continue seamlessly over time.
Start a new conversation by giving it a brief topic.
Zulip empowers remote and flexible work.
Check out these case studies to see the impact.
Work better together
The first-class threads in Zulip are
absolutely critical to how we work
. So many people on Hacker News talk about using Zulip — I'm so glad we joined them!
David Lanstein
,
co-founder and CEO of Atolio
Empower leaders
Using Zulip significantly
increases the size of the team
for which a manager can meaningfully know what’s going on.
Gaute Lund
,
co-founder of iDrift AS
Make decisions faster
Some decisions that were blocked for months on
                                GitHub were
resolved within 24 hours
on Zulip.
Josh Triplett
,
Rust Language team co-lead
Simplify knowledge management
Using Zulip in a way that feels natural creates
                                an
organized repository
of knowledge
as a
                                side effect
.
Max McCrea
,
Co-founder of Monadical
Organize 100s of workstreams
Other apps like Slack would struggle with
                                organizing the flow of information in a complex
                                organization like ours. Zulip’s UI makes it easy
                                to
access all the information you need
.
Jon Jensen
,
CTO of End Point
                                Dev
Communicate with efficiency
I don’t like going back to Slack now. It’s just
                                not as efficient a way to
organize
                                communication
.
James van Lommel
,
Director of Engineering at Semsee
You own your data
Escape corporate vendor lock-in. You can
self-host
Zulip’s 100%
open-source
software for full data sovereignty, or start
                            with a convenient
cloud
                            solution
and
move
any time.
We make it easy to switch from
Slack
,
Teams
and
other
                            tools
.
Curious to learn more?
Dive into our detailed guide for organizations like yours.
Business
Organizations from small businesses to enterprises communicate more efficiently.
Research
For your group, lab, department or scientific field.
Education
Communication hub for classes, in-person or online.
Open-source
Grow and engage your community.
Non-profits, Governments
Free or highly-discounted plans are available for most non-business uses.
Events and conferences
For organizers and attendees at your conference, workshop, or hackathon.

On this page
Overview

Mastering Zulip
Other resources
Zulip help center
Welcome to the help center for
Zulip organized team chat
!
Here, you’ll find up-to-date information on how to use Zulip, or administer a
Zulip organization. These pages are designed to guide you as you learn your way
around Zulip. They’ll also be your go-to if you’re ever unsure how something
works, or if you need to explain it to someone else.
Guides for new users
Section titled “Guides for new users”
The following guides will help you get started, and more can be found in the
left sidebar.
Getting started with Zulip
Choosing a team chat app
Why Zulip
Trying out Zulip
Zulip Cloud or self-hosting?
Moving to Zulip
Mastering Zulip
Section titled “Mastering Zulip”
If you’re using Zulip a lot, it’s worthwhile to learn workflows that will help you
spend that time efficiently. Check out:
Keyboard shortcuts
: Mastering a few keyboard
shortcuts (like
N
to go to the next unread topic) can speed up your
workflows.
Reading strategies
: Learn different ways to
find what you want to read.
Mastering the compose box
: Learn about
everything Zulip’s compose box lets you do.
Message formatting
: Learn about
quotes, spoilers, global times, and more.
Search filters
: Learn how to find
messages based on where they were sent, whether they contain links or
attachments, and other details.
Other resources
Section titled “Other resources”
You may also want to check out:
An overview
of the features available in Zulip.
Installation
instructions
for setting up a self-hosted Zulip server, and documentation on
running Zulip
in production
.
API documentation
for writing integrations or bots
using the Zulip API.
Contributing
guide
,
with step-by-step instructions on how to get started contributing code to
Zulip.
If you have any questions that aren’t answered here,
let us
know
!
We work hard to make Zulip’s help center comprehensive and easy to follow.
Please drop by the
#documentation
channel in the
Zulip development
community
to share ideas for how to
make it better.
Your feedback helps us make Zulip better for everyone! Please
contact us
with questions, suggestions, and feature requests.

hits.title: Zulip 4.0: Threaded open source team chat
Zulip 1.8: Free software Slack alternative with email-style threading
Zulip 3.0: Threaded Open Source Team Chat
Zulip Server 1.9: HipChat import and much more

analysis.name: Zulip
analysis.value_proposition: A topic-threaded team chat built for async communication, available as open-source software you can self-host or use in the cloud.
analysis.target_segment: enterprise / mid-market / dev / education / open-source communities
analysis.ideal_customer: Distributed or async-first teams—engineering orgs, universities, research groups, and open-source communities—who need structured conversations that don't require real-time presence

features.name: topic-based threading
features.description: Every message belongs to a named topic inside a channel, creating persistent, navigable conversation threads
features.evidence_quote: The first-class threads in Zulip are absolutely critical to how we work.

features.name: channels (streams)
features.description: Named channels for organizing conversations by team, project, or subject area
features.evidence_quote: Introduction to channels — Subscribe to a channel, Create a channel, Pin a channel

features.name: inbox view
features.description: Overview of all conversations with unread messages across channels and DMs
features.evidence_quote: Get an overview of conversations with unread messages in your inbox.

features.name: recent conversations view
features.description: Chronological list of recently active conversations for quick navigation

features.name: combined feed view
features.description: Single feed showing all messages across channels in time order

features.name: direct messages
features.description: One-on-one and group direct messaging outside of channels

features.name: user groups
features.description: Named groups of users for bulk mentioning and permission management
features.evidence_quote: Create user groups — User groups, View group members

features.name: custom groups (flexible permissions)
features.description: Granular permission sets assignable to custom-defined user groups

features.name: guest users
features.description: Restricted-access accounts for external collaborators with configurable channel visibility
features.evidence_quote: Guest accounts — Limit user list access for guests

features.name: role-based permissions
features.description: Multi-role user hierarchy including admins, moderators, members, guests with granular controls

features.name: message search with filters
features.description: Full-text and filter-based search across message history
features.evidence_quote: Search filters — Searching for messages, Scroll to date

features.name: message history limit (10k messages free)
features.description: Free cloud tier limits searchable message history to 10,000 messages

features.name: unlimited message history
features.description: No cap on searchable message history on paid cloud tiers

features.name: message retention policies
features.description: Configurable automatic deletion of messages after a set period

features.name: markdown formatting
features.description: Rich text message composition with markdown including bold, italic, code, links
features.evidence_quote: Message formatting — Text emphasis, Paragraph and section formatting

features.name: latex support
features.description: Inline and block LaTeX math rendering in messages

features.name: code blocks
features.description: Syntax-highlighted fenced code blocks in messages

features.name: tables
features.description: Markdown table rendering in messages

features.name: polls
features.description: Interactive polls creatable inline within messages

features.name: collaborative to-do lists
features.description: Shared checklist widget embeddable in messages for team task tracking

features.name: spoilers
features.description: Collapsible hidden content blocks in messages

features.name: /me action messages
features.description: IRC-style /me status action messages

features.name: global times
features.description: Time references in messages that auto-display in each reader's local timezone

features.name: emoji reactions
features.description: React to messages with emoji

features.name: custom emoji
features.description: Upload custom emoji for the organization

features.name: animated gifs
features.description: Inline animated GIF support in messages

features.name: file sharing
features.description: Upload and share files within messages

features.name: file storage (5gb free / 5gb per user standard)
features.description: Cloud storage for uploaded files with per-org or per-user caps by tier
features.evidence_quote: File storage up to 5 GB total … File storage up to 5 GB per user

features.name: file storage (25gb per user plus)
features.description: Expanded file storage at 25GB per user on Plus tier
features.evidence_quote: File storage up to 25 GB per user

features.name: saved snippets
features.description: Reusable text snippets for composing repetitive messages quickly

features.name: draft messages
features.description: Save message drafts before sending

features.name: scheduled messages
features.description: Compose a message and send it at a specified future time

features.name: reminder scheduling
features.description: Set reminders on messages to resurface them later

features.name: read receipts
features.description: See when recipients have read a direct message

features.name: typing notifications
features.description: See when another user is composing a message

features.name: message preview before sending
features.description: Render message formatting before sending to verify appearance

features.name: message edit and delete
features.description: Edit or delete previously sent messages with configurable restrictions
features.evidence_quote: Edit a message — Delete a message — Restrict message editing

features.name: message edit history
features.description: View the full edit history of a message
features.evidence_quote: View a message's edit history — Restrict message edit history access

features.name: move messages between topics/channels
features.description: Reorganize messages by moving them to a different topic or channel
features.evidence_quote: Move content to another channel — Move content to another topic

features.name: topic resolve
features.description: Mark a topic as resolved to indicate a conversation is complete
features.evidence_quote: Resolve a topic — Restrict resolving topics

features.name: topic follow
features.description: Follow specific topics to receive notifications for new activity

features.name: muting channels/topics/users
features.description: Suppress notifications and hide content from specific channels, topics, or users
features.evidence_quote: Mute or unmute a channel — Mute or unmute a topic — Mute a user

features.name: do not disturb
features.description: Temporarily silence all notifications

features.name: email notifications
features.description: Configurable email digests and mention notifications

features.name: desktop notifications
features.description: Native OS desktop push notifications

features.name: mobile notifications
features.description: Push notifications to iOS and Android apps; unlimited for eligible communities
features.evidence_quote: Mobile notifications without limitation for eligible communities … for up to 10 users for other organizations

features.name: weekly digest emails
features.description: Weekly summary email of activity in the organization

features.name: star messages
features.description: Bookmark messages for personal reference

features.name: message via email (email gateway)
features.description: Send messages to Zulip channels by emailing a special address
features.evidence_quote: Message a channel by email — Using Zulip via email

features.name: channel folders
features.description: Group channels into collapsible folders in the sidebar

features.name: private channels
features.description: Invite-only channels not visible to non-members

features.name: public access option
features.description: Allow channels to be read by non-logged-in users for open communities

features.name: custom profile fields
features.description: Add organization-specific fields to user profiles

features.name: custom linkifiers
features.description: Auto-link patterns (e.g. issue numbers) to external URLs

features.name: call integration
features.description: Start video/audio calls with configurable provider from within chat
features.evidence_quote: Start a call — Configure call provider

features.name: keyboard shortcuts
features.description: Comprehensive keyboard shortcuts for navigation and message composition

features.name: dark theme
features.description: Dark mode UI theme
features.evidence_quote: Dark theme — Select theme: Dark / Light / Auto

features.name: desktop apps
features.description: Native desktop apps for Windows, macOS, and Linux

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native iOS and Android mobile applications

features.name: sso
features.description: Single sign-on support; SAML on Plus/Business, OpenID Connect on Enterprise

features.name: saml authentication
features.description: SAML-based SSO with providers like Okta and OneLogin

features.name: openid connect sso
features.description: OpenID Connect SSO support for enterprise self-hosted deployments

features.name: scim provisioning
features.description: SCIM 2.0 user and group lifecycle management for automated provisioning

features.name: ad/ldap user sync
features.description: Sync users and groups from Active Directory or LDAP
features.evidence_quote: AD/LDAP user sync … AD/LDAP and SAML group sync

features.name: custom domain
features.description: Use a custom subdomain for your Zulip Cloud organization

features.name: organization branding
features.description: Replace Zulip logo with your own brand logo

features.name: compliance data exports
features.description: Full and compliance-grade data exports for regulatory needs

features.name: import from slack
features.description: Migrate full message history and users from Slack
features.evidence_quote: Import from Slack — Moving from Slack

features.name: import from microsoft teams
features.description: Migrate full message history and users from Microsoft Teams
features.evidence_quote: Import from Microsoft Teams — Moving from Microsoft Teams

features.name: import from mattermost
features.description: Migrate full message history and users from Mattermost

features.name: import from rocket.chat
features.description: Migrate full message history and users from Rocket.Chat

features.name: import from discord
features.description: Migrate from Discord into Zulip

features.name: organization export
features.description: Export full organization data for backup or migration
features.evidence_quote: Export your organization — Zulip Cloud to self-hosting — Self-hosting to Zulip Cloud

features.name: self-hosting
features.description: Run Zulip on your own servers with full data control; 100% open-source
features.evidence_quote: You can self-host Zulip's 100% open-source software for full data sovereignty

features.name: open-source code
features.description: Entire server, web, and mobile codebase is open-source on GitHub

features.name: rest api
features.description: Full REST API for programmatic access to all Zulip functionality

features.name: incoming webhooks
features.description: Receive and post messages from external systems via webhook URLs

features.name: interactive bots
features.description: Build and deploy interactive bots that respond to commands

features.name: 130+ native integrations
features.description: Out-of-the-box integrations across CI, monitoring, project management, and more
features.evidence_quote: Over 130 native integrations. And hundreds more through Zapier and IFTTT.

features.name: zapier integration
features.description: Connect Zulip to thousands of apps via Zapier
features.evidence_quote: And hundreds more through Zapier and IFTTT.

features.name: ifttt integration
features.description: Connect Zulip to apps via IFTTT automations
features.evidence_quote: And hundreds more through Zapier and IFTTT.

features.name: wildcard mentions
features.description: Mention @all, @everyone, or @channel to notify all subscribers; configurable restriction

features.name: user status and availability
features.description: Set custom status messages and show presence/availability

features.name: moderation toolkit
features.description: Tools for community moderators including report message and moderation requests
features.evidence_quote: Community moderation toolkit — Enable moderation requests — Report a message

features.name: restrict direct messages
features.description: Admin control to restrict who can send direct messages

features.name: require topics
features.description: Enforce that all channel messages must have a topic

features.name: image and video previews
features.description: Inline rendering of linked images and video embeds

features.name: hide message content in emails
features.description: Option to redact message body from notification emails for confidentiality

features.name: communities directory
features.description: Public listing of open Zulip communities for discovery
features.evidence_quote: Communities directory — Linking to your organization

features.name: demo organizations
features.description: Spin up a temporary demo organization without creating an account
features.evidence_quote: Demo organizations — Anonymously if you like

features.name: priority commercial support
features.description: Faster, dedicated support response for paid cloud tiers

features.name: implementation consulting
features.description: Hands-on deployment and migration consulting for enterprise self-hosted customers

features.name: custom feature development
features.description: Zulip team builds custom features for enterprise self-hosted customers

features.name: sponsorship and discounts program
features.description: Free or heavily discounted plans for open-source, academic, non-profit, and community orgs

features.name: migrate between cloud and self-hosted
features.description: High-quality export/import tooling to move freely between Zulip Cloud and self-hosted
features.evidence_quote: You can move freely between Zulip Cloud hosting and your own servers with our high quality export and import tools.

features.name: channel posting policy
features.description: Restrict who can post to a channel (e.g. announcements-only channels)

features.name: configure who can start new topics
features.description: Restrict topic creation within a channel to specific roles or groups
features.evidence_quote: Configure who can start new topics

features.name: default channels for new users
features.description: Automatically subscribe new members to specified channels
features.evidence_quote: Set default channels for new users

features.name: quote and forward messages
features.description: Quote a message in a reply or forward it to another conversation
features.evidence_quote: Quote or forward a message — Format a quote

features.name: link to messages and conversations
features.description: Generate permanent links to individual messages, topics, or channels
features.evidence_quote: Link to a message or conversation — Link to a channel, topic or message

features.name: collapse messages
features.description: Collapse a long message to reduce visual clutter

features.name: restrict new member permissions
features.description: Limit what newly joined members can do until they've been in the org for a set time

features.name: deactivate and reactivate users
features.description: Suspend user accounts without deleting their message history

features.name: configure authentication methods
features.description: Enable/disable email, social, SAML, or LDAP login methods

features.name: font size and line spacing customization
features.description: User-adjustable font size and line spacing preferences

features.name: full width display
features.description: Toggle full-width message display for wider screens

features.name: emoticon translations
features.description: Auto-convert text emoticons like :) to emoji

features.name: proxy support
features.description: Connect through an HTTP proxy for restricted network environments

features.name: custom certificate
features.description: Use a custom TLS certificate for self-hosted deployments

differentiators: Topic-based threading as a first-class primitive — every message belongs to a named topic, enabling true async without losing context
100% open-source server, web, and mobile clients with self-hosting option
Move freely between Zulip Cloud and self-hosted with high-quality export/import tools
Nonprofit ownership (Zulip Foundation) — no corporate vendor lock-in
Sponsors 1500+ organizations with free or deeply discounted plans for open-source, academic, and non-profit use
Knowledge repository as a side-effect: natural Zulip usage creates an organized, searchable archive of decisions
Email-style threading model that scales to hundreds of simultaneous workstreams without chaos

praised: "The first-class threads in Zulip are absolutely critical to how we work." — David Lanstein, Atolio
"Using Zulip significantly increases the size of the team for which a manager can meaningfully know what's going on." — Gaute Lund, iDrift AS
"Some decisions that were blocked for months on GitHub were resolved within 24 hours on Zulip." — Josh Triplett, Rust Language team
"Using Zulip in a way that feels natural creates an organized repository of knowledge as a side effect." — Max McCrea, Monadical
"I don't like going back to Slack now. It's just not as efficient a way to organize communication." — James van Lommel, Semsee
Hacker News community reception consistently positive across multiple major version launches (v1.8, v3.0, v4.0, v5.0 all 350–755 upvotes)
Threading model praised as a Slack alternative built for async and distributed teams

free_tier.limits: 10,000 messages search history; 5 GB total file storage; mobile notifications up to 10 users

tiers.name: Free (Cloud)
tiers.headline_inclusions: 10k message history, 5GB total storage, complete team chat, 100+ integrations, advanced roles/permissions, guest accounts

tiers.name: Standard (Cloud)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited history, 5GB/user storage, custom groups, message retention, org branding, public access, priority support

tiers.name: Plus (Cloud)
tiers.headline_inclusions: All Standard + 25GB/user storage, SAML SSO, SCIM sync, custom domain, enhanced guest controls; 10-user minimum

tiers.name: Free (Self-hosted)
tiers.headline_inclusions: All Zulip features; mobile notifications unlimited for eligible communities, up to 10 users otherwise

tiers.name: Basic (Self-hosted)
tiers.headline_inclusions: All Zulip features, unlimited mobile notifications, supports open-source development; $20/month off first year

tiers.name: Business (Self-hosted)
tiers.headline_inclusions: All features, unlimited mobile notifications, SAML/LDAP/AD support, compliance exports, data retention; 25-user min

tiers.name: Enterprise (Self-hosted)
tiers.headline_inclusions: All features + OpenID Connect, LDAP/SAML group sync, SCIM, phone support, implementation consulting, custom dev

pricing.notes: Business (self-hosted) monthly rate is $8/seat. Plus (cloud) monthly rate is $12/seat. Non-profits, open-source projects, academic institutions, and developing-world orgs eligible for free Standard cloud or 85%+ discounts. 25-user minimum on self-hosted Business; 10-user minimum on cloud Plus. First-year $20/month discount on self-hosted Basic and Business.

recent_hn.title: Zulip 12.0: Organized chat for distributed teams
Ask HN: Does anyone here use Discord as their work chat tool?
RustChat is a minimal team messenger, alternative to Slack, Mattermost, Zulip
